title: anthemis maritima subsp. secundiramea
intro: Anthemis maritima subsp. secundiramea, also known as a subspecies of the sea chamomile, is a perennial plant that belongs to the daisy family Asteraceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Anthemis maritima subsp. secundiramea include reducing inflammation, calming digestive issues, and promoting relaxation. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and topical creams, which can be used to treat a variety of health conditions.
